envelope_defaults.human_readable_summary: GitHub connector readiness substrate per ADR-0084. Project / Engineering pack family. Prefer GitHub App over OAuth App pattern for installation-level auth + finer permissions + higher rate limits.
envelope_defaults.model_usage_notes: Suggest-only. Activation requires Foundation governance + map-region approval + per-connector RULE 21 research arc completion.

items.MCP_posture: Official GitHub MCP server exists (github/github-mcp-server). Substrate-honest evaluation deferred to a later C-slice pre-flight. Initial path may be MCP server OR direct GitHub App with fine-grained installation tokens.
items.OAuth_model: Two patterns: (1) GitHub App (preferred) — authenticates at the installation level, supports fine-grained permissions, 15,000 API requests/hour per installation; (2) OAuth App — authenticates as a user, less granular, 5,000 requests/hour. NIOV-preferred: GitHub App.
items.app_installation_model: GitHub App created at github.com/settings/apps. Installed per organization / per user with explicit repository selection. Installation token retrieved via JWT-signed app credential exchange.
items.admin_consent_model: Organization owner installs GitHub App + selects repositories. Per-repository permission grant captured at install. Enterprise organizations can require app review at the enterprise level.
write_capabilities: issues:write (issue create / update / comment — disabled by default)
pull_requests:write (PR create / review / comment — disabled by default)
contents:write (file commits — disabled by default; code-modification risk)
actions:write (workflow run triggers — disabled by default)
administration:write (repo settings — disabled by default; admin scope)
webhook_or_event_capabilities: GitHub webhooks for issue / PR / push / workflow_run / installation events
Webhook signature verification via x-hub-signature-256 header (HMAC SHA-256 with shared secret) — direct fit for verifyInboundHmac substrate
Per-installation webhook delivery + replay via GitHub admin UI for missed events
required_approval_gates: Admin policy approval at activation per ADR-0084
Per-call governance approval for any write action
dual_control_recommendations: contents:write to default branch — dual-control mandatory
PR merge — dual-control recommended for sensitive repos
Workflow run triggers on production-deploy workflows — dual-control
DMW_scope_implications: Repository code / issues / PRs scoped to ENTERPRISE DMW (engineering domain knowledge)
Per-repository scope: sensitive repos (security / infra / customer-data) excluded by Risk Map
Public-repo content treated as public-source; private-repo content treated as same-org-only
PR review state + check state treated as workflow-coordination DMW
Author identity scoped to engineering team boundary
workflow_purpose_bindings: ADR-0081 Stage 2: PR review summary recommendations
ADR-0081 Stage 2: CI failure analysis recommendations
ADR-0081 Stage 3: Drafted PR review comments awaiting human approval
ADR-0081 Stage 3: Drafted issue triage updates awaiting human approval
GitHub as workflow trigger: incoming webhook events → recommendation pipeline
items.billing_pack_mapping: Project / Engineering Pack family per ADR-0083 Amendment 1 §9.4 (connector-pack.project-engineering.v1).
Dandelion_map_dependencies: Workflow Map must approve GitHub-bound workflows (PR review / triage / release)
Authority Map must approve write authority per repository tier
Memory / DMW Map must approve repository-level memory scope
Risk Map must clear sensitive repositories (security / infra / customer-data excluded by default)
audit_expectations: Per-installation + per-repository scope captured in audit metadata
secret_handling_requirements: GitHub App private key (PEM) per environment via secret_ref env-var-NAME per ADR-0019 + ADR-0024
Webhook shared secret per installation via secret_ref
OAuth App client_secret (if OAuth path chosen) via secret_ref
Installation token cached per-installation with short TTL; auto-refresh via app-JWT exchange
no_leak_rules: Repository file content / issue body / PR description never traverses responses outside the wallet's scope
GitHub user identity (login + email) never exposed cross-tenant
Repository names / owner names never exposed cross-tenant
GitHub App private key never logged
tenant_isolation_requirements: Per-tenant GitHub installation (GitHub App installed per organization)
Multi-tenant GitHub App pattern requires per-installation token scoping
ConnectorBinding enforces wallet_id + entity scope on every API call
items.rate_limit_notes: GitHub Apps: 15,000 req/hr per installation (REST); GraphQL has separate 5,000-point/hr quota + repository bonus. Secondary rate limits: 900 points/min REST + 2,000 points/min GraphQL. OAuth Apps: 5,000 req/hr (lower; reason GitHub App is preferred).
testing_strategy: Replay-mode integration tests using recorded GitHub responses
Webhook x-hub-signature-256 verification tests against verifyInboundHmac
No-leak guard tests (no source code / tokens / secrets in responses)
implementation_risks: Confused-deputy via installation-level auth: GitHub App acting across repositories needs per-call repository attribution in audit
Secondary rate limits hit harder than primary — implementation must surface secondary backoff
Repository-content writes are production-effect — keep disabled until late C-slice + dual-control
Default-branch protection-bypass scenarios — must respect branch protection rules
Public-vs-private repository scope confusion — explicit scope classification required
items.first_slice_recommendation: C-GitHub read-first connector runtime — GitHub App installation with metadata + contents + issues + pull_requests + checks read scopes. Webhook ingestion via x-hub-signature-256 + verifyInboundHmac. NO writes. Evaluate official github-mcp-server before direct API integration. After C2 Slack + GOVSEC.6.
